name: Bali: Organic Blue Moon, Kintamani Highlands
cupping_notes: Smooth and syrupy body with notes of baker's chocolate, cedar, molasses, and vanilla.
description:
A home roaster’s secret and a hidden jewel, our Bali Blue Moon’s birthplace has been heavily regulated by the Indonesian island’s government since the 1970s, which is a bittersweet situation––on the one hand, farmers may have less individual control over their crop. However, it also means the quality, handling, and processing are consistently excellent. Moreover, Bali experiences significant social, economic, agricultural, and technological enhancement because of this regulation. No matter what viewpoint you have, it can’t be denied that this complex bean is of high quality. 

Cupping Notes: Smooth and syrupy body with  Smooth and syrupy body with notes of baker's chocolate, cedar, molasses, and vanilla.

Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Brazil: Fazenda Bela Vista, Sul de Minas
cupping_notes: Fazenda Bela Vista Washed Yellow Bourbon has notes of red fruit, mild acidity, and a creamy body.
description:
Bela Vista Farm: Excellence in South Minas Coffee


Situated in the south of Minas, Bela Vista farm thrives in a distinctive micro-region known for producing exceptional highland coffee. This family-owned, traditional estate combines time-honored knowledge with cutting-edge agricultural techniques to achieve a consistent and recognizably high-quality coffee. The farm uses rigerous cultivation practices while maintaining complete traceability throughout the production process.


Beyond coffee production, Bela Vista demonstrates its community commitment by funding a primary school serving both workers' children and the surrounding rural community.
 

Cupping Notes: Fazenda Bela Vista Washed Yellow Bourbon has notes of red fruit, mild acidity, and a creamy body.
 
Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Brazil: Oberon, Cerrado
cupping_notes: a creamy body, sweet chocolate and citric notes, and a light acidity. Cupping notes: Chocolate, nutty, fruity aftertaste; light acidity, sweet, full body. Clean and balanced.
description: Brazil’s Oberon coffee is a pure Cerrado blend that perfectly captures what the region is known for in one sip––blissful temperatures averaging 68 degrees Fahrenheit, altitudes reaching just below 1,250, and an average rainfall of 1,600 millimeters ––a haven for healthy coffee trees. The Cerrado land houses just over 140,000 hectares of coffee production that vary in size from 100 hectares to 3,000––all dedicated to consistent cup quality. Cupping notes: Chocolate, nutty, fruity aftertaste; light acidity, sweet, full body. Clean and balanced. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Brazil: Pearl Bourbon Peaberry, Daterra Estate
cupping_notes: A harmonious coffee with pronounced sweetness in the aftertaste. Try a medium/medium dark roast to bring out distinctive nuttiness with a hint of soft fruit and low acidity.
description: These small peaberry beans are filled with aromatic and flavor of fruit, molasses and caramel. An extremely harmonious coffee with pronounced sweetness in the aftertaste. An ideal drip coffee and espresso component. Try a medium/medium dark roast for an enhanced flavor that brings out its distinctive nuttiness with a hint of soft fruit with low acidity. Daterra Estate is one of the world-famous farms in Brazil where the coffee is produced with an inspiring commitment to both quality and sustainability. All Daterra Farms coffees have been Rainforest Alliance certified since 2003. Cupping Notes: Fruity, molasses, and caramel with a nice, sweet aftertaste. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: CBC Blend: High Octane Robusta Blend
cupping_notes: Rich, low acidity with notes of dark chocolate, vanilla, and cinnamon with a medium to heavy body and bold, earthy flavors with a big caffeine jolt.
description: Rich, low acidity with notes of dark chocolate, vanilla, and cinnamon with a medium to heavy body and bold, earthy flavors with a big caffeine jolt.

name: CBC Blend: Organic Big Black
cupping_notes: This bold organic coffee blend brings out the best of African and Asian coffee beans. This blend has a lot of body with both earthy and chocolaty notes - a balanced blend with a touch of spice.
description: This bold organic coffee blend brings out the best of African and Asian coffee beans. African coffees are usually described as complex, fruity and floral. These are stronger, fragrant-rich, and full-bodied flavored beans. Asian coffees tend to be earthier and darker than most other regional blends. These coffees tend to be smooth, dense, and exotic with dark chocolate, bold nuttiness, and subtle earthy tones. We think this coffee is best as a dark roast, but your taste will be the final decider! Let us know how you roasted this variety in the product reviews.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Colombia: El Gato Supremo, Bucaramanga
cupping_notes: Well-balanced with sweet aromas of brown sugar and maple syrup. Smooth, silky medium body carries bright, crisp winey acidity, complemented by citrus, lemon, orange, red fruit, nectarine, and dark chocolate notes. A clean, lingering finish with sweet, rich complexity.
description:
El Gato Supremo is grown through the Santander Department, which is widely known for its natural, shade-grown coffees. These beans are cultivated in rich forests where volcanic soils, idyllic microclimates, and average temperatures between 68 and 71 degrees Fahrenheit are ideal for growing specialty-grade arabica.
 

A proper 17/18 screen Colombia Supremo green coffee, this bean is handpicked, washed, and milled by the Northeastern Colombian Coffee Cooperative (Cooperativa Cafetera Del Nororiente Colombiano).
 
Cupping Notes: Well-balanced with sweet aromas of brown sugar and maple syrup. Smooth, silky medium body carries bright, crisp winey acidity, complemented by citrus, lemon, orange, red fruit, nectarine, and dark chocolate notes. A clean, lingering finish with sweet, rich complexity.

Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Colombia: Reserva Del Patron, Narino
cupping_notes: Excellent body, deep, rich. complex and bright. Fruity (currants and plums) overtones with a nice chocolate background, just a hint of earthiness and a long, satisfying finish.
description:
The quintessential Colombian coffee, La Minita’s Reserva del Patrón hails from Nariño, the southernmost district of Colombia. This coffee is “cherry-picked” (pardon the pun) from specific growing conditions exclusively in high-altitude plantations––if coffee does not exceed 1600 meters, it is not considered for export. Because of this process, only 6,000 of La Minita’s 150,000 bags are Reserva del Patrón and make it to the highest Europrep standards produced yearly.If you have loved Don Enrique, del Patron is great replacement!

Cupping Notes: Bright, fruity, and well balanced. Excellent body, deep, rich, complex and bright. Fruity (currants and plums) overtones with a nice chocolate background, just a hint of earthiness and a long, satisfying finish.

Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Colombia: Supremo EP, Huila
cupping_notes: Well balanced acidity and a delicate medium body. It starts off sweet with notes of tart fruit and finishes with a bittersweet, dark cocoa notes.
description:
A 17/18 screen (Supremo), the Colombia Huila is smaller-bodied when compared to its relative, the Bucaramanga. Grown and harvested in Huila, Colombia, this bean’s origin lies near the Nevado de Huila volcano––the soil is rich with minerals, and the microclimate is perfect for perfect, complex coffee. The producers use manual, hand-cranked machines to de-pulp the bean’s cherry, and raised beds dry the coffee evenly. The beans are expertly handled at every processing stage

Cupping Notes: Well balanced acidity and a delicate medium body. It starts off sweet with notes of tart fruit and finishes with a bittersweet, dark cocoa notes.

name: Costa Rica: El Conquistador, Tarrazu
cupping_notes: Fruity, nutty characteristics that make this region’s coffees famous. Brightness, body, complexity, aroma and good balance are all clearly apparent.
description: These Costa Rican coffee beans come from one of the exceptional coffee farms in the Tarrazu region. You get a premium Tarrazu at a non-premium price from Hacienda La Minita. This arabica bean is a classic Tarrazu exhibiting the fruity, nutty characteristics that make this region's coffees famous. Brightness, body, complexity, aroma and good balance are all clearly apparent. It has the classic piquant acidity and lively winey flavor expected of the region’s best coffees. Cupping Notes: Well balanced with notes of lush tropical fruit and bittersweet chocolate and has a smooth pronounced body. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Costa Rica: Finca Corralar Microlot, Los Santos
cupping_notes: Lime, plum, orange, and blackberry notes with dark chocolate depth; syrupy body, bright fruit-forward acidity; complex fruit sweetness with rich finish.
description:
This Rainforest Alliance Certified natural processed coffee comes from Finca Corralar, a microlot produced by the renowned Hacienda La Minita in Costa Rica's Los Santos region. Los Santos, one of Costa Rica's premier coffee-growing areas, provides ideal high-altitude conditions and volcanic soils that create exceptional flavor development in this carefully selected microlot.


Hacienda La Minita is recognized for its rigorous quality standards and commitment to sustainable agriculture, with Rainforest Alliance certification reflecting their dedication to environmental stewardship and responsible farming practices. The natural processing method enhances the coffee's inherent fruit-forward characteristics, allowing the sugars from the coffee cherry to fully penetrate the beans during the extended drying period.


The microlot designation indicates this coffee represents a small, exceptional batch that has been separated and processed independently to showcase its unique terroir and processing characteristics. The combination of bright citrus (lime and orange), stone fruit (plum), berry (blackberry), and rich dark chocolate creates remarkable complexity, while the syrupy body provides structure for the vibrant fruit flavors—demonstrating why Costa Rican naturals are gaining recognition alongside the country's traditionally washed offerings.

Cupping Notes: Lime, plum, orange, and blackberry notes with dark chocolate depth; syrupy body, bright fruit-forward acidity; complex fruit sweetness with rich finish.
Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Costa Rica: La Gladiola Peaberry, Tarrazu
cupping_notes: Toasted almond, citrus, and dried cherry notes; balanced acidity with nutty sweetness; clean, bright finish.
description:
This Costa Rican peaberry coffee comes from La Gladiola in the renowned Tarrazu region, one of Costa Rica's most prestigious coffee-growing areas. Peaberries are a natural mutation where only one bean develops inside the coffee cherry instead of the typical two, creating a rounder, denser bean that offers unique flavor compounds and complexity.


The Tarrazu region's high altitude, volcanic soils, and ideal microclimate create perfect conditions for cultivating exceptional coffee. La Gladiola's commitment to quality processing ensures that these rare peaberry beans are carefully separated and processed to showcase their unique characteristics.


The combination of toasted almond richness, bright citrus acidity, and dried cherry sweetness demonstrates the classic Costa Rican flavor profile elevated by the peaberry's natural concentration. This coffee offers both the clean brightness that has made Costa Rican coffee world-famous and the added complexity that makes peaberries sought after by roasters and coffee enthusiasts alike.

Cupping Notes: Toasted almond, citrus, and dried cherry notes; balanced acidity with nutty sweetness; clean, bright finish.
Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Costa Rica: La Gladiola, Tarrazu
cupping_notes: La Gladiola exhibits a wonderful acidity, good body with some citrus cocoa notes.
description: If you like La Magnolia, you will fall in love with La Gladiola! The fruit that comprises La Gladiola comes from the Western portion of the Tarrazu valley. The fruit is purchased from specific farmers, process it at La Minita's mill, Beneficio del Rio Tarrazu and kept separate from their other trademark coffee beans. Cupping Notes: Wonderful acidity, good body with some citrus and cocoa notes.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Costa Rica: La Magnolia, Tres Rios
cupping_notes: Cupping Notes: Sparkling, clean, lemon-orange brightness with chocolate and nutty overtones that carry through to the long, complex finish
description: What a fitting name for this wonderfully sweet and aromatic coffee! The La Magnolia is one of the finest Tres Rios coffees we've ever offered, brought to you by the same people who bring you the La Minita Terrazu.The La Magnolia is a beautifully prepped, remarkably well-rounded, exceptionally well-balanced, complex coffee. If you're in search of a special Costa Rican coffee (and even if you're not), this may be the one.

name: Costa Rica: Organic La Amistad, Coto Brus
cupping_notes: Brown sugar sweetness with milk chocolate and a fruity orange finish. Expect a full-bodied zing in your cup!
description: Hacienda la Amistad, located in Coto Brus, a canton in the province of Puntarenas, Costa Rica, is owned and operated by Roberto Montero, a third-generation coffee farmer. Roberto’s grandfather first came to the area in the early 1900's as part of a team surveying the border between Costa Rica and the newly formed country of Panama. This organic coffee embodies all the good qualities you look for in a high-grown Costa Rican. Cupping notes: Brown sugar sweetness with milk chocolate and a fruity orange finish. Expect a full-bodied zing in your cup! Try before you buy with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: El Salvador: Finca La Esperanza, Santa Ana
cupping_notes: Milk chocolate, brown sugar, cherry, with medium acidity, a bold body, and above-average sweetness.
description:
Grown in the western part of El Salvador within the Apaneca Ilamatepec mountain range where the fertile land is perfect for coffee beans, this new variety is grown on the Magana-Menendez family farm – 1,000 meters above sea level. With over 100 years of experience, the struggle for sustainability and social function has been the Magana-Menendez family’s main focus, and this attention to detail helps produce their high-quality coffees and preserve the coffee’s ecosystem with the Rainforest Alliance.
 

Cupping Notes: Milk chocolate, brown sugar, cherry, with medium acidity, a bold body, and above-average sweetness.
 
Try before you buy with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Ethiopia Mokamba Natural
cupping_notes: Cupping Notes: Pungent berry notes, winey acidity, juicy, and dark chocolate
description: The Mokamba is a blend of coffees from three regions, mainly a coffee from the Kambata region (known for the production of ginger) blended with special preparation Sidamo. Patio dried from ripe cherry, the two green coffees are carefully balanced to create a harmonious natural. Cupping Notes: Pungent stone fruit notes, winey acidity, juicy, dark chocolate, and spice. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Ethiopia: Decaf Sidamo Natural, Sidamo
cupping_notes: Above average sweetness, medium body & brightness, with notes of milk chocolate, cherry, and brown sugar.
description:
This Ethiopia Decaf is a Naturally processed coffee bean from Sidamo -- one of the zones of the Oromia Region of Southern Ethiopia. 
 

Cupping Notes: Above average sweetness, medium body & brightness, with notes of milk chocolate, cherry, and brown sugar.

Try before you buy with a 1/2lb sample!

name: Ethiopia: Durato Bombe, Sidamo
cupping_notes: Very bright and very sweet, expect notes of watermelon, strawberry, Meyer lemon, and a floral nose.
description: In 2006, Asefa Dukamo and his brother Mulugeta founded Daye Bensa Coffee within the Sidama region. With 16 washing stations and 4 dry mills, their coffee is processed in a timely, efficient manner. Hard work and meticulous processing at the Qunqa mill occur, and the whole cherries are sun-dried for 15 days at most––this creates a sweet, complex profile. The Durato Bombe coffee is collected from 626 farmers in its namesake village and the Bensa Woreda Sidama zone. In fact, the Durato Bombe also shares its name with the mountain it originates and grows on! Cupping Notes: This coffee from the Bombe Mountain has a lot of body for a naturally processed heirloom coffee. Very bright and very sweet, expect notes of watermelon, strawberry, Meyer lemon, and a floral nose. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Ethiopia: Kembata G1, Durame Village
cupping_notes: Meyer lemon, dark chocolate, and blueberry notes with vibrant fruit character; high acidity, high body; above average sweetness with bright citrus and rich berry complexity.
description:
This Ethiopian natural coffee comes from the Durame Village of Kembata at 2050 meters above sea level, where extremely fertile soil creates ideal conditions for growing high-quality Ethiopian Heirloom varietals. The high altitude and rich volcanic soil contribute to the coffee's exceptional flavor development and complexity.


The natural processing method incorporates a unique 36-48 hour fermentation process in the wet mill before the cherries are moved to raised beds for sun drying. The unwashed cherries spend 16-17 days drying under the Ethiopian sun, allowing the fruit sugars to fully penetrate the beans and create the intense fruit-forward profile characteristic of this processing method.


Harvested in November and exported in April, this Grade 1 coffee showcases the exceptional terroir of the Kembata region. The combination of high altitude, fertile soil, and careful processing creates a cup that balances bright citrus acidity with rich chocolate depth and vibrant blueberry sweetness, demonstrating why Ethiopian naturals are considered among the world's most distinctive coffees.
 

Cupping notes: Meyer lemon, dark chocolate, and blueberry notes with vibrant fruit character; high acidity, high body; above average sweetness with bright citrus and rich berry complexity.
 
Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Ethiopia: Misty Valley, Yirgacheffe
cupping_notes: dark berry, chocolate, pomegranate, floral, clean, milky body, incredible balance throughout.
description:
Ethiopia prepares some of the world’s finest coffee, known for complexity and delicious flavor profiles you can’t find anywhere else. From roasting connoisseurs to casual coffee lovers, our customers adore the versatility of this bean. Find out why this coffee always sells out!
 
Producer, exporter, and cultivator Abdullah Bagersh ensures that Misty Valley’s ripe cherries are subject to superior care from start to finish. Through the first 48 hours, the coffee beans are constantly turned to provide even evaporation. This process provides consistency and cleanliness, which is often difficult to accomplish in the Gedio region of Ethiopia. The resulting speak for themselves! 

Cupping Notes: dark berry, chocolate, pomegranate, floral, clean, milky body, incredible balance throughout.

name: Ethiopia: Organic Dimtu Tora, Guji
cupping_notes: Balanced and exotic with chamomile florals, citrus, and red fruit notes; a rich, smooth body and a clean, sweet finish with mild tangy acidity.
description:
Experience the elegance of Ethiopia’s famed Guji region with this organic coffee from Dimtu Tora in Hambela Wamena. Grown at elevations of 1800–2200 meters above sea level, the cool highland climate allows the coffee cherries to mature slowly, developing exceptional depth, clarity, and sweetness.


Produced by smallholder farmers, this coffee carries forward generations of Oromo tradition. Cultivated from regional landraces and heirloom varieties, it reflects the biodiversity and craftsmanship that make Guji coffees so distinctive. The fertile soils and high elevation of Hambela contribute to a cup profile that is both vibrant and beautifully balanced.


From the first aroma to the lingering finish, this coffee is layered and expressive. A sweet floral fragrance of chamomile and vanilla is complemented by delicate black tea tones. On the palate, expect a well-balanced and exotic flavor profile featuring bright citrus notes of orange and lemon, intertwined with red fruit and cherry sweetness. The cup finishes clean, with a sweet, fruity-floral aftertaste that lingers pleasantly.


Cupping Notes: Balanced and exotic with chamomile florals, citrus, and red fruit notes; a rich, smooth body and a clean, sweet finish with mild tangy acidity.

name: Ethiopia: Organic Konga G2, Yirgacheffe
cupping_notes:
Bergamot, peach, orange marmalade, tea-like but silky body. Delicate 
and articulated.
description:
The Konga cooperative is one in a network of cooperatives in the Yirgacheffe region belonging to the Yirgacheffe Coffee Farmers Cooperative Union (YCFCU). Its 2,400 smallholder members distinguish themselves with their impeccable attention to detail in processing. 
Ethiopian heirloom varietals are chosen from the forest and transplanted to small gardens, adding a layer of selection to the incredible complexity available among wild coffee plants. 
Farmers hand-sort for unripe and overripe cherries. Coffee is pulped with a 5-disc Agaarde pulper and wet fermented for 24 to 36 hours. After sorting based on density, the fermented coffee is washed in clean water before being dried for one to two weeks on cloth drying beds. Workers cover the coffee with plastic at midday and at night. Once dried, the coffee is screened 14 and up. 
As part of the cooperative's mission to give back to its community, it recently renovated buildings at a primary school and outfitted it with new supplies. 

Cupping Notes: Bergamot, peach, orange marmalade, tea-like but silky body. Delicate 
and articulated.
Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Ethiopia: Organic Limu G2, Oromia
cupping_notes: Fruity with limey citrus, herbal, and floral notes. Chocolaty and complex.
description:
This grade 2 Ethiopian coffee from the Limu region is one of the most interesting coffees we've come across recently, and for an Ethiopian coffee, that's saying something! We cupped one sample and immediately knew we had to bring this one in. 



Jimma zone is located to the southwest of Ethiopia’s capital, Addis Ababa. Much of Jimma, like other coffee-growing regions in Ethiopia, is a vast, gently sloping plateau with incredibly fertile soil at high elevations. The top-quality coffees from Jimma, especially those from the Limu, Gera, and Goma districts, are renowned for their vibrant and delicate fruit flavors. The region’s renowned "white" honey, collected by bees from the blossoms of coffee plants and the surrounding forests, is a well-known symbol of Jimma and brings immense pleasure to the senses. Coffees from this zone, along with those from the nearby Illubabor region, are often labeled as “Limu”, a distinction based more on terroir and shared flavor profiles rather than geographical boundaries.



Cupping notes: Fruity with lime citrus, herbal, and floral notes. Chocolaty and complex.

name: Ethiopia: Queen City, Harrar
cupping_notes: Aroma with hints of cherry, dark chocolate, and dried fruit. The taste features the same flavor notes, and is overtly wild and earthy with excellent balance.
description: Harrar is known as "the coffee of kings," grown in the original coffee country and dried naturally on the trees. This adds a wildness to the cup which is also spicy, fruity and flowery, quite complex. This coffee is versatile, with something good to offer across the entire roast spectrum. Some compare it to Mocca; I call it a work of art...with an attitude. The aroma will get your attention first: hints of cherry, dark chocolate, and dried fruit (pairs VERY well with a nice granola!). The taste is what your nose has prepared you for, but overtly wild and earthy with excellent balance. The outstanding fruitiness is still there in great measure, but the lower-tones of chocolate and earth really compliment it more than any Harar in recent memory. Its brightness really shines while it's still hot but does ebb as the temperature cools, especially in darker roasts. This coffee is very versatile. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Guatemala: Finca Santa Margarita, Acatenango
cupping_notes: Smooth body, jasmine, floral, cocoa powder, milk chocolate, almonds, caramel, delicate finish.
description: Finca Santa Margarita is the family farm of Camila Topke, one of our trading partners and a member of the International Women’s Coffee Alliance. In May, I visited Guatemala and learned firsthand the challenges Guatemalan producers are experiencing. Founded in 1838 by Camila’s great grandfather, Thomas Wyld, Santa Margarita is now 2,320 hectares––812 of which are coffee farms with Caturra, Sarchimor, Catimor, and Geisha varieties. Santa Margarita is committed to serving the community and environmental sustainability––the surrounding natural forest and wildlife are preserved. The farm houses an elementary school for children, a church, and a health clinic on-site. The majority of the farm’s operators are women––from coffee pickers to those running the grafting program, school, and health clinic. Cupping notes: Smooth body, jasmine, floral, cocoa powder, milk chocolate, almonds, caramel, delicate finish. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Guatemala: HHT SHB, Huehuetenango
cupping_notes: Well balanced intense flavor, sweet brown sugar honey aroma, smooth medium body and clean sweet lingering aftertaste with bright crisp vibrant acidity, citrus, orange, lime, peach, apple and chocolate notes.
description:
Huehuetenango sits in the western highlands of Guatemala, bordering Mexico. It was one of the most ethnically diverse regions in Guatemala, and coffee growing has a rich, deep history and ties to the land. This variety is a proven, popular coffee you shouldn't miss!
 

Cupping Notes: Well balanced intense flavor, sweet brown sugar honey aroma, smooth medium body and clean sweet lingering aftertaste with bright crisp vibrant acidity, citrus, orange, lime, peach, apple and chocolate notes.

Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Guatemala: La Flor Del Cafe Peaberry, Antigua
cupping_notes: It has lively acidity and a heavy body with flavors of chocolate and caramel.
description: This Peaberry comes from the Pastores mill in the heart of Antigua up to 1,600 meters above sea level on small farms. It is the same version of our La Flor del Cafe, only the peaberries are sorted out. Coming from one of the world's top coffee growing regions, Guatemala, the quality control is impeccable, with only the absolute cream of the crop chosen for export. Cupping Notes: Lively acidity and a heavy body with flavors of chocolate and caramel. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Guatemala: La Flor Del Cafe, Antigua
cupping_notes: The aroma is rich, dark, chocolate, classic on the nose. Acidity is low to medium and the body is full. The flavor is subtle: dark, mocha notes, and rich.
description:
Our Antigua bean is washed, sun-dried, and hand-sorted in the modernized Pastores mill up to 1,600 meters above sea level on small farms. Coming from one of the world’s top coffee-growing regions––Guatemala––the quality control is impeccable, with only the absolute cream of the crop chosen for export. It’s one of the most sought-after coffees in Antigua because it can handle a variety of roast styles, it has a lively, fruity flavor profile, and the perfect amount of acidity. 
Cupping Notes: The aroma is rich, dark, chocolate, classic on the nose. The acidity is low to medium, and the body is full. This is a very balanced cup. The flavor is subtle, dark, mocha notes, and rich.Try before you stock up with a 3lb or 5lb sample!!

name: Haiti: Blue Pine, Central Plateau
cupping_notes: Three hand chosen varietals that provide smooth coffee, with balanced natural sweetness and floral notes. Low in acidity, sweet undertones with floral aromas
description: This coffee is from APCAB Coop which has a total of 680 growers. APCAB coffees are known for being smooth and creamy and are Shade Grown which is a rarity in Haiti to have that kind of forest cover. The cooperative is paid roughly 300% higher wages than the Fair Trade minimum, not out of charity, but for having an exceptional product. Cupping Score: 86 Cupping Notes: Hand chosen varietals that provide smooth coffee, with balanced natural sweetness and floral notes. Clean smooth body. Low in acidity, and sweet undertones. Floral aromas.  Learn more about Haitian coffee beans with our coffee guide!

name: Haiti: Marre Blanc, Central Plateau
cupping_notes: Three hand chosen varietals that provide smooth coffee, with balanced natural sweetness and floral notes. Heavy almond and peanut flavors, buttery mouthfeel, smooth floral acidity. Strong sweet undertones bring a balanced creamy body.
description: This coffee is from Coopcab which has three sub cooperatives at different elevations. Blue Pine, Marre Blanc, and Gwo Chwal. Each sub cooperative has around 400 members. Coopcab is the largest cooperative in Haiti, and typically sells coffee to Japan. This coffee is Shade Grown which is a rarity in Haiti, as most areas are much deforested. The President of Coopcab, oversees all coffee processing to ensure consistency for each of the sub cooperatives. Our trading partner, Café Kreyol, partners directly with the cooperative, but also has their own personnel on the ground in these areas to ensure sustainability and quality control on the farm and cooperative level. Cafe Kreyol is currently working with the cooperative to assess the profitability of members/ growers to ensure further financial sustainability. Cupping score: 87 Cupping notes: Hand chosen varietals that provide smooth coffee, with balanced natural sweetness and floral notes. Heavy almond and peanut flavors, buttery mouthfeel, smooth floral acidity. Strong sweet undertones bring a balanced creamy body.  Learn more about Haitian coffee beans with our coffee guide!

name: Haiti: Organic Haitian Blue, Norde
cupping_notes: Mellow, smooth and creamy body, super nutty, almonds, and as a buttery mouthful. It has a low acidity, medium complexity, and a sweet, smooth finish.
description: Haitian Blue Organic is grown off the same type of plant as the Jamaican Blue Mountain coffee. This one comes from one of the oldest coops in Haiti, Coopacvod. This coop strictly grows Organic and shade grown coffee. The coop's President, Francis Dubois found a way to use local river water to power the plant and recycle the water to put back into those rivers. Not only has the coop found a way to sustain the local water sources, but has created sustainable employment for citizens of Haiti. Cupping Notes: Mellow, smooth, and creamy body, super nutty, almonds, a buttery mouthful. It has a low acidity, medium complexity, and a sweet, smooth finish. A flawless cup!Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!  Learn more about Haitian coffee beans with our coffee guide!

name: Haiti: Savanne Zombie, Central Plateau
cupping_notes: Clean, smooth body, low acidity, and sweet undertones. Floral aromas.
description: Haitian Zombie was roasted and sent in by our trader and scored 92 in Coffee Review! This Haitian Coffee is grown in a town called Zombie Desert, and it has one of the only model farms in the area. This cooperative, being smaller is size, is able to produce 600 bags of coffee and pay growers significant prices for their coffee cherries. This coffee is Shade Grown which is a rarity in Haiti to have that kind of forest cover. The cooperative is paid roughly 300% higher wages than the Fair Trade minimum, not out of charity, but for having an exceptional product. It is grown by a Coop in the Central Plateau region consisting of 75 farmers where the average size of a farm is 1 hector. Cupping notes: Clean smooth body. Low in acidity, and sweet undertones. Floral aromas.  Learn more about Haitian coffee beans with our coffee guide!

name: Hawaii: Kona Peaberry, Mauka Honaunau District
cupping_notes: Rich, smooth taste with mild acidity, creamy body, and a light lingering aftertaste with notes of milk chocolate, vanilla, caramel, nuts, and citrus.
description: We are excited to add this new offering to our lineup this year from Aloha Farms Hawaii- a group of Kona coffee farms located on the big island of Hawaii. Their mission is to promote sustainable coffee practices and provide opportunities for Hawaiian families and farms. Their commitment to sustainable agriculture and regenerative coffee growing produces an excellent product. Aloha Farms Hawaii are the founders of the largest co-op of Kona coffee farms with on-site processing and milling. The coffee is grown in the golden corridor which consists of coffee farms that stretch for 22 miles along the old Mamalahoa Highway where the coffee grows in the shade of Mount Hualaloa. The Peaberry is considered their "Champagne of Kona coffee"- their rarest and most sought-after coffee. An anomaly in the growing process, the Peaberry is the only bean fully developed in the coffee cherry. Normally, there are two beans that grow in each cherry, and they press together within the fruit causing the flat and rounded beans we all know. With the Peaberry, that second bean is absent all together, which results in one oval bean. Peaberry coffees have a greater density of flavor, sugars, and lipids compared to regular coffee beans. Peaberry coffees are rare, and normally only make up around 3-7% of an entire crop. There is extra labor and time involved in the sorting process which results in 100% Kona Peaberry of the highest quality. Cupping Notes: Rich, smooth taste with mild acidity, creamy body, and a light lingering aftertaste with notes of milk chocolate, vanilla, caramel, nuts, and citrus. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Hawaii: Kona Purple Mountain, Honaunau District
cupping_notes: Sweet and chocolatey with fruity undertones
description: This is the first Kona that we have sold that was farmed organically. This is the pure, classic Kona, in its big bean size. Grown at 2000 feet elevation and hand picked then sun dried. This coffee exhibits the farmer's love of the island, giving you the classic aroma and body of a fine Kona. Cupping Notes:  Sweet and chocolatey with fruity undertones. Roast it until it glistens, let it cure, enjoy the cup, and appreciate the beauty of the beans. Aloha. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: India: Cherry Robusta
cupping_notes: Malt, popcorn, buckwheat tea, and cocoa
description:
This Robusta coffee is sourced from the Sakleshpu and Coorg districts in Karnataka, India. Coffee is produced on family-owned farms, many of which have been owned by the same family for generations.
Caffeine content is higher in Robusta coffees than in Arabica and the Robusta leaves are larger with deep roots and higher yields. if you're looking for a Natural processed Robusta, this is the one!
Cupping Notes:  Malt, popcorn, buckwheat tea, and cocoa

name: India: Mysore Nuggets, Chikmagalur District
cupping_notes: Bakers chocolate, graham cracker, and nutmeg.
description: Sourced from the southwestern Chikmagalur district––the coffee capital of India––the Mysore coffee bean produces rich flavor and a decadent finish, which is the standard for more than 70% of coffees produced near Karnataka. However, the India Mysore is washed unlike its monsooned relatives––the Mysore is simply de-pulped and dried with very little fermentation. Cupping Notes: Bakers chocolate, graham cracker, and nutmeg. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Jamaica: Blue Mountain Peaberry, Portland Parish
cupping_notes: Chocolate, sweet, floral, and creamy aroma, with dark chocolate, slightly herbal, nutty, floral, mild fruits, a pop of spice & honey. Mild earthy and dark chocolate aftertaste
description:
Jamaican Blue Mountain peaberry coffee beans are among the rarest and most flavorful coffee beans in the world today. Only 5% of the total JBM harvest is peaberries, so getting to roast these isn’t just a real treat - it’s a collector’s item for serious coffee lovers!



Roasting Tips:

 Roast in small batches to ensure even heat distribution.
Monitor the roast color carefully — peaberry beans roast slightly faster.
Use a good-quality home coffee roaster or a cast-iron skillet for best results.

 
Cupping Notes: Chocolate, sweet, floral, and creamy aroma, with dark chocolate, slightly herbal, nutty, floral, mild fruits, a pop of spice & honey. A mild earthy & dark chocolate aftertaste.
Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Jamaica: Blue Mountain, Clydesdale
cupping_notes: Aromas and flavors of dried apple, mellow tobacco, baking spice, whole milk body.
description: This JBM is from the Clydesdale region which is located in the parish of St. Andrew. The producer is the Jamaican Coffee Growers Association and is a collection of small farmers or microlots who cooperate in order to bypass the larger estate processing system. High altitudes of 3000-5000ft above sea level with ample rainfall and volcanic soil provides a unique growing environment unlike anywhere else. Cupping notes: Aromas and flavors of dried apple, mellow tobacco, baking spice, whole milk body. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Java: Blawan Estate, East Java
cupping_notes: A rich, medium-acidity coffee with a robust body and sweetness, with notes of vanilla, caramel, and black cherry.
description:
The Kayumas Estate, or "Golden Wood", is located on the island of Java in Indonesia and was first established by the Dutch in the 18th century. This Estate is situated in the northeast area of the Bondowoso province of Java. Initially, coffee was cultivated in low-lying areas, but in the 19th Century, coffee leaf rust disease destroyed most of their production, forcing new coffee cultivation into the highland plateaus where higher altitudes and volcanic soil provided perfect growing conditions.
 

The Kayumas coffee is grown in East Java, on a government-run estate where the coffee is handpicked and then processed using the fully washed method – this gives the coffee a cleaner profile than other Indonesian coffees.
 

Cupping Notes: A rich, medium-acidity coffee with a robust body and sweetness, with notes of vanilla, caramel, and black cherry.


Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Java: Organic Taman Dadar, Kayumas
cupping_notes: Above average sweetness and body with low acidity and notes of vanilla, dark chocolate, and cedar.
description:
Sourced from small, family-owned farms in the Surya Abadi Kayumas cooperative, this coffee is given its hallmark Indonesian profile through a unique process known as wet-hulling – the coffee parchment is removed before drying is completed.
 

On the island of Java, Indonesia, coffee production is regularly dominated by government-run estates, a hierarchy first established in the 18th century. Be that as it may, this particular bean was refined by only 66 producers with an average farm size of 5 acres.
 

Cupping notes: Above average sweetness and body with low acidity and notes of vanilla, dark chocolate, and cedar.
 
Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Kenya: Peaberry Plus 15/16, Kirinyaga County
local_region: Meru, Embu, Nyeri, Muranga, Kirinyaga, Kiambu, Kangundo
harvest: September- December (Main Crop); May- July (Fly Crop)
cupping_notes: Clean and well balanced with a smooth medium body, crisp and vibrant acidity and a sweet brown sugar aroma with flavor notes of orange, blackberry, grapefruit and nectarine.
description: This new Kenyan Peaberry Plus is grown in rich, volcanic soil with ample rainfall which is an ideal climate for the development of the aroma and flavor notes that this country's coffees are known for. The PB Plus refers to the smaller screen size, 15/16 and the quality of this bean. Most farms in this part of Kenya are found near the second tallest mountain in Africa- Mt. Kenya. Each country in this region has a number of small farms that work together to make up local grower association and cooperatives as well as larger estates which gives the country a diverse production base. Cupping Notes: Clean and well balanced with a smooth medium body, crisp and vibrant acidity and a sweet brown sugar aroma with flavor notes of orange, blackberry, grapefruit and nectarine. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Mexico: La Laja Honey, Tlaltetela, Veracruz
cupping_notes: Supremely sweet with a mild orange citric acidity and a smooth, creamy body. Very clean and consistent.
description:
La Laja, a family farm and mining group in Veracruz, Mexico, began in 1920 when its founder, Hermilo Sampieri, bought his first coffee farm in Huatusco. His son joined in, and eventually, one farm became seven––Finca La Laja, Finca El Mirador, Finca Las Palmas, Finca La Veracruz, and three other farms in Puebla. 
La Laja also purchases a wide variety of cherries from small farmers in Chiapas. They are experts in processing, and they own wet and dry mills. La Laja’s honey-processed bean has its skin removed, but the sugary pulp remains through fermentation to add a sweet flavor. After de-pulping, this coffee dries for up to 20 days on covered raised beds. 
Cupping notes: This coffee is supremely sweet with a mild orange citric acidity and a smooth, creamy body. It is very clean and consistent.
Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Mexico: Organic Mocabe HG, Chiapas
cupping_notes: Washed and sun dried. Cups with notes of dark chocolate, a slight citrus sweetness, some nuttiness and a well-rounded body.
description: Our certified organic Mexican Chiapas coffee comes from small growers in mainly the mountainous Soconusco region. The growers partner with a certified mill in Tapachula, who not only mills the coffee but cups and evaluates the end result, taking great pride in eliminating any defects that can affect the final cup. Roasters who purchase organic coffee want to know their coffee preserves and protects the environment, but they also desire a high quality cup that will stand out above all else. Our Soconusco, Chiapas organic Mexican coffee is washed and sun dried. Cupping Notes: Notes of dark chocolate, a slight citrus sweetness, some nuttiness and a well-rounded body. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Mexico: Turquesa HG, Chiapas
cupping_notes: Milk chocolate, nutty, grape, citrus; medium body, cools sweet
description:
This lot comes from a group of smallholder producers in Northern Chiapas. The coffee is brought to the San Cristobal de las Casas and Yajalón buying stations in Northern Chiapas. These producers plant, harvest, and prepare the coffee by hand. Generally, each produces between 10 to 15 bags. To help ensure the producers receive a fair price, InterAmerican works closely with the exporters that support these communities with social projects. The exporters also use their own transportation infrastructure to bring the producers’ coffees to market.

The harvest season is December through March. All the coffee is received in parchment, quality controlled, and transported to a high-tech dry mill in Veracruz. After a second quality check, the coffee is cleaned, milled and sorted to be prepared for export. This coffee has a quality-control standard that allows for only 15 imperfections per 300 grams.

Cupping notes: Milk chocolate, nutty, grape, citrus; medium body, cools sweet.
Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Multi Origin: Decaf Morning Glory, Multi Origin Blend
cupping_notes: This outstanding decaf is a multiple-origin blend named in honor of that lovely blue flower that blooms before sunrise, is vibrant all day and calls it quits by sundown. Citrus, bright, good body, milk chocolate sweetness, balanced, less decaf taste.
description: Morning Glory Swiss Water Process Decaf will become one of our best selling Decafs! A unique blend of South & Central American coffees with a hint of Indonesian coffees decaffeinated using the Swiss Water Process. This outstanding decaf is a multiple-origin blend named in honor of that lovely blue flower that blooms before sunrise, is vibrant all day and calls it quits by sundown. You might just say this coffee is for folks who are all about some shut eye, come nightfall. Morning Glory Decaf is decaffeinated by Swiss Water Process, which uses water to create a coffee that’s 99.9 percent caffeine free.  Cupping notes: Citrus, bright, good body, milk chocolate sweetness, balanced, less decaf taste. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Nicaragua: Las Segovias Coop SHG, San Juan del Rio Coco
cupping_notes: Clean well balanced flavor, smooth medium body, sweet brown sugar aroma, sweet aftertaste and bright crisp acidity with apple, lemon, pear, peach and chocolate notes.
description:
Specialty coffee in Nicaragua is built around small farm cooperatives, supporting each other and helping to maintain high quality from seed to ship! And the producers in San Juan del Rio Coco is no different. These producers use traditional organic farming practices on small plots of land, where they cultivate coffee alongside shade trees, bananas, corn, and beans.


During the harvest, each producer engages in selective picking, focusing on harvesting only fully ripened cherries, which are then processed using the farm's micro-mill. The cherries undergo washing and floating to remove damaged and less dense coffee beans. The selected cherries are then de-pulped, fermented, washed, gently dried, and stored until they are ready for export.


Cupping Notes: Clean well balanced flavor, smooth medium body, sweet brown sugar aroma, sweet aftertaste and bright crisp acidity with apple, lemon, pear, peach and chocolate notes.


Try before you stock up with a half-pound sample!

name: Panama: Janson Farms Geisha, Volcan High Lands
cupping_notes: Balanced, juicy, crisp, mild, and smooth with flavor notes of milk chocolate, caramel, orange blossom, lemon, black cherry
description: Panama Geisha- 531 This Panama Geisha comes from the Janson Coffee Farm which is located in the Talamanca Mountain Range near the Tisingal and Baru volcanoes in Volcan, Panama. It is a family owned Hacienda that was established in 1941 by Carl Axel Janson. The farm's main focus is quality production. No pesticides, no herbicides! The balance of nutrients in the soil comes from using enzyme micro organisms. This farm is committed to improve and protect the environment by using solar panels to produce the energy for the farm, reusing all excess matter from processing for fuel and fertilizer, and seeds from the forests are planted in the nursery for reforestation and protection of water resources. Natural spring water is used for their Washed processing. The Janson's processing plant was built in 1993. It was designed specifically for specialty coffee. They do Natural and Washed processing with updated technology added to artisanal methods to achieve the quality you will experience in this delicious bean! Cupping notes: Balanced, juicy, crisp, mild, and smooth with flavor notes of milk chocolate, caramel, orange blossom, lemon, black cherry Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Papua New Guinea Organic Korofeigu AX
cupping_notes: Robust, Earthy, cedar, ginger, syrupy body, spicy Mexican chocolate.
description: Korofeigu Farmer's Cooperative Society is located in the Bena Bena valley of the Eastern Highlands between Goroka to the west and Henganofi to the east. The Coop is comprised of 97 members, with a total cultivation area of 112 hectares. The mountainous, moist cultivation area has loamy soil and a variety of native shade trees. .  Cupping Notes: Robust, Earthy, cedar, ginger, syrupy body, spicy Mexican chocolate.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Papua New Guinea: Mile High A, Eastern Highlands
cupping_notes: Bright acidity and creamy body with notes of berries, caramel, malt, and citrus.
description: The Mile High Papua New Guinea beans are grown in part by the Arokara Cooperative––a plantation partner with firm beliefs in chemical-free coffee production and updates to their processes annually. The Mile High is grown on the outskirts of Kainantu, which is a town in the Eastern Highlands of Papua New Guinea. Nearly 12,000 of Kainantu’s citizens depend on coffee processing to support their families, and as many as 60 assistants can be hired at one time. The farms sit at an altitude of 1,600 meters above sea level, and their sizes are modest. The Mile High beans are highly consistent and “clean” due to their washed nature––the cherries are de-pulped and hardly fermented. This way, any overbearing sweetness disappears. Cupping notes: Bright acidity and creamy body with notes of berries, caramel, malt, and citrus. Try before you buy with a 1/2lb sample!! Learn more about the coffee beans grown in Papua New Guinea with our guide.

name: Peru: Organic Decaf Sol Y Cafe, Cajamarca
local_region:
Cajamarca, in the provinces of Jaen
and San Ignacio
cupping_notes: Notes of milk chocolate and caramel with hints of orange and a creamy body.
description: Cooperativa de Servicios Multiples Sol y Café Ltda (Coop Sol y Café) was established in March of 2008 to unite over 1,000 farmers. This included rice and cacao producers. The co-op was originally formed to collectively make agronomic and economic decisions. One of their objectives was to protect the environment, and to help communities and their families to develop and progress. They continue to work towards the sustainability of their crops and development for the future by maintaining the Organic and Fair Trade certifications.  Cupping Notes: Notes of milk chocolate and caramel with hints of orange and a creamy body - roasted full medium.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Peru: Organic Finca La Esperanza G1, San Martin
cupping_notes: Chocolate & cherry, toasty, nutty, and sweet aroma. Notes of citrus, malt, pine, and semi-sweet chocolate with a mild, lingering aftertaste, low acidity, full-bodied, and well-balanced.
description: Shade-grown and younger than six years old (within its first few seasons of production), the Finca La Esperanza is sourced from a 2.5-acre family farm in San Martin’s Soritor village. The producer, Walter Gutierrez, purchased the once-abandoned Finca La Esperanza in 2007 and transformed it into a marvelous spot for Peru organic coffee to flourish. The Finca La Esperanza coffee is grown alongside corn and cocoa, proving the previous owner wrong and showing anything is possible. Finca La Esperanza’s cup profile has only improved with the farmers’ ever-growing education and organic certification throughout the years.Coffee is Peru’s most prominent agricultural commodity, and it is the ninth-largest arabica producer in the world. Peru is also an organic coffee hotspot––there are 220,000 organic coffee farms––and high elevations with just the right amount of shade in the Northern Andes. Since farmers traditionally use all-natural methods when growing their beans (natural fertilizer, chemical/pesticide avoidance, and recycling coffee pulp, mulch, and other substances), it was only fitting that they strive for organic coffee excellence. Because of their efforts, the area is known as a sanctuary for birds and other docile wildlife in the balanced ecosystem, and San Martin’s rainforest is abundant with fertile soil. Cupping Notes: Chocolate & cherry, toasty, nutty, and sweet aroma. Notes of citrus, malt, pine, and semi-sweet chocolate with a mild, lingering aftertaste, low acidity, full-bodied, and well-balanced.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Peru: Organic La Florida, Selva Central
cupping_notes: Milk Chocolate, Pear, Toffee, with a full, creamy body, and mild acidity.
description:
The La Florida Cooperative was established in 1965 by a group of small-scale farmers from the Chanchamayo region–since then, the organization has been committed to improving its coffee beans. For the past 13 years, La Florida has not only been dedicated to producing the highest quality beans but also strives to improve their quality of life – both for their people and the environment. The organization’s Fair Trade revenue brings education, healthcare, and infrastructure to an otherwise rural area – in fact, the Occupational Learning Center for Sustainable Agriculture was created due to their efforts.
 

Cupping Notes: Milk Chocolate, Pear, Toffee, with a full, creamy body, and mild acidity.

name: Rwanda: Organic Kawa Yacu Peaberry, Karongi District
cupping_notes: Delicate and refined, this coffee offers a black tea-like body accented by bright lemon acidity and sweet, juicy peach notes.
description:
Discover a coffee with incredible character and an even more powerful story. This special lot from the Women Coffee Extension at Rwanda’s Mbizi Coffee Washing Station is produced by a cooperative of more than 700 women farmers dedicated to quality, sustainability, and community growth.


Founded in 2017, the Women Coffee Extension was created to give female coffee producers a stronger voice in Rwanda’s specialty coffee industry. Their commitment to better farming practices and member support has already led to higher yields, expanded production, and stronger livelihoods for families across the region.


Choosing this coffee means offering your customers more than a great cup—it means sharing a product with purpose. From agricultural training and nutrition programs to healthcare support and future plans for Rainforest certification, this coffee represents meaningful impact at origin and a bright future ahead.


Rich in story and rooted in empowerment, this is a standout Rwanda selection your customers can feel good about buying and brewing.


Cupping Notes: Delicate and refined, this coffee offers a black tea-like body accented by bright lemon acidity and sweet, juicy peach notes.

name: Sulawesi:Toraja Sapan Minanga, Tana Toraja
cupping_notes: Low acidity and sweetness, heavy body, with notes of vanilla, molasses, and black licorice.
description:
The Torajan tribe inhabits the central mountains of South Sulawesi province, preserving traditional customs including their distinctive boat-shaped houses. This region features dramatic landscapes where humid lowland rice paddies meet towering thousand-foot rock faces shrouded in mist. Coffee thrives at elevations up to 2000 MASL, among the highest-growing regions in Indonesia.


Small-scale farmers, typically working less than 3 acres each, have recently formed cooperative micro-mills to enhance processing quality. Their meticulous approach includes careful cherry sorting, overnight fermentation after de-pulping, washing, and initial patio drying. What distinguishes these coffees is the unique wet-hulling process (locally called "Giling Basah"), where parchment is removed while beans still retain high moisture content. This traditional method gives Indonesian coffee its characteristic bluish hue and distinctive flavor profile.


To overcome challenging local infrastructure and transportation limitations, producer groups have established partnerships with regional exporters. These collaborations provide crucial logistical support and milling facilities, enhancing traceability and quality control throughout post-harvest processing. Such partnerships also facilitate quicker market access internationally, enabling producers to benefit more directly from specialty coffee trade relationships.
 

Cupping Notes: Low acidity and sweetness, heavy body, with notes of vanilla, molasses, and black licorice.
 

Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Sumatra Lake Toba, Lintong TP
cupping_notes: Triple picked and wet hulled, with a smooth, full body and flavor notes of sweet herbs and rich earthiness.  Medium body, cumin spice, basil, roasted green pepper, complex acidity and aroma
description:
Grown by local producers on the biodiverse plateau of Lintong, southwest of Lake Toba, this exceptional Sumatra coffee is the result of generations of care and craft. Partnering with a trusted mill, every step—from hand-picking to the rare triple-pick process—is executed with precision. The beans are wet-hulled, sun-dried, and sorted by hand for quality, producing a distinctive blue-green hue and a cup that reflects true dedication.

Cupping Notes: Triple picked and wet hulled, with a smooth, full body and flavor notes of sweet herbs and rich earthiness.  Medium body, cumin spice, basil, roasted green pepper, complex acidity and aroma

Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Sumatra: Mandheling G1, Aceh
local_region: Batak Region of West-Central Sumatra, Aceh
cupping_notes: Cupping Notes: Chocolate, cedar, sweet tobacco, and spice, along with a mild acidity and creamy body.
description: This shade-grown Sumatran coffee is a classic, so steeped in the history of this Indonesian island that the name itself is not of a region, but rather the result of a language barrier. Sumatra Mandheling is grown on the slopes of Mount Leuser, a volcano near the port of Pandang, in the Batak area of Aceh. It is home to one of the most ancient ecosystems in the world! The flavor profile is vibrant and spicy, a heavy coffee reminiscent of the atmosphere on this tropical paradise. If you're looking for a Sumatran coffee that won't let you down, this is a go-to. Cupping Notes: Chocolate, cedar, sweet tobacco, and spice, along with a mild acidity and creamy body. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Sumatra: Organic Decaf Mandheling, Aceh
cupping_notes: Having almost as much body as the high-octane Sumatras, it provides a nice, clean cup with the complexities found in other Sumatrans.
description: Sourced from family-owned farms located in the Gao highlands of Aceh (Sumatra, Indonesia), this bean is grown in volcanic soil, sun-dried, and wet-hulled; and it is led by the export company CV Gayo Mandiri Coffee––a family operation with access to their own mill, success helping surrounding farmers access international markets, and promoting earnings with a direct trade. Cupping Notes: Average body, acidity, and sweetness with notes of cedar and dark chocolate. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Sumatra: Organic Mandheling, Aceh
cupping_notes: Medium bodied cup with bright acidity and "clay" earthiness. Notes of orchid and orange and barest grapefruit. Light in the mouth but not insubstantial, with very light aftertaste.
description: Sumatra is Indonesia's second-largest island, and it’s filled with mineral-rich soil and diverse plant life. Grown in the shady slopes of Mount Leuser––near Pandang in the Batak area of Aceh––the Sumatra Mandheling coffee bean is born in the most mineral-rich land available in the world––volcanic soil. In addition to coming from an ideal coffee-growing environment, this bean cultivates in one of the world’s most ancient ecosystems. In fact, its namesake is the result of a language barrier in the culturally-diverse climate. Cupping Notes: A medium bodied cup with bright acidity and the typical "clay" earthiness that marks the region. Complexity is refined with notes of orchid and orange and barest grapefruit. Pleasingly light in the mouth but not insubstantial, with very light aftertaste.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Timor: Organic Decaf Cafe Timor Coop, East Timor
cupping_notes: Low acidity, heavy body with notes of dark chocolate, raisin, and molasses.
description: Our regular/full caffeine Timor beans have gained such popularity, we jumped on this new Timor water-processed (no chemicals) decaf for those who enjoy a bold Indonesian coffee with almost no caffeine. Cupping Notes:Cupping Notes:Low acidity, heavy body with notes of dark chocolate, raisin, and molasses.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Timor: Organic Heritage Reserve FTO, East Timor
cupping_notes: Pleasant, tangy acidity, a full body with a robust mouthfeel, and elevated sweetness with distinct notes of dark chocolate and graham cracker.
description:
Introducing our Timor Heritage Reserve FTO coffee, a product of the cooperative efforts of 2,172 dedicated farmers from Aileu, Ermera, and Liquica in Timor. This coffee, grown on small farms averaging slightly over a hectare each, flourishes at altitudes ranging from 1,000 to 1,400 meters above sea level.

This cooperative farming effort aims to ensure sustainable practices while improving both the quantity and quality of their coffee.

Timor Heritage Reserve FTO presents a delightful cupping experience. It showcases a pleasant acidity, providing a subtle tang, along with a full body for a robust mouthfeel. Elevated sweetness with distinct notes of dark chocolate and graham cracker, forming a comforting and indulgent experience.

With every sip of Timor Heritage Reserve FTO, you not only enjoy a quality cup of coffee but also support the hard work and dedication of these enterprising farmers. It's a testament to their commitment to their craft and the rejuvenation of their community. Cupping Notes: Pleasant, tangy acidity, a full body with a robust mouthfeel, and elevated sweetness with distinct notes of dark chocolate and graham cracker.

name: Uganda: Mt Elgon A, Bugisu
cupping_notes: Sweet floral brown sugar vanilla honey aroma, well balanced intense flavor, clean sweet fruity rich aftertaste, smooth medium body and bright crisp winey acidity with citrus, lemon, orange, lemon, lime, peach, pear and pomegranate notes

name: Uganda: Organic Bugisu, Sipi Falls
cupping_notes: A complex cup with notes of baker's chocolate, molasses, and black pepper. Minimal brightness, above average sweetness, and a pleasing, full body.
description: A coffee bean like no other grows on the verdant slopes of Africa’s oldest volcano, Mount Elgon––the Ugandan Organic Bugisu. Family farms source this bean, and it’s named after the people native to the area: the Bugisu. The Sipi Falls Coffee Project has been regulating this bean and providing support since 1999 and has evolved to protect over 2,000 hectares of production. Agronomic practices have constantly improved over the years in order to yield more coffee at its best quality through intercropping, which allows the beans to have the vital nutrients they need. Cupping Notes: A complex cup with notes of baker's chocolate, molasses, and black pepper. Minimal brightness, above average sweetness, and a pleasing, full body. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Vietnam: Robusta, Central Highlands
cupping_notes: A heavy-bodied Robusta offering with a stout body, balanced cup with a clean finish.
description: Vietnam has become the world's largest robusta producer, and the second-largest coffee producer. The area most known for this production is the southern Central Highlands region. Robusta beans are known by their name because they are full-bodied, robust beans with a higher caffeine content than arabica. Robusta beans contain generally 265.00 mg per 8.00 fl. oz cup which is extremely high! Most robusta beans are bought for their high caffeine content and the longer-lasting crema! Cupping Notes: A heavy-bodied Robusta offering with a stout body, balanced cup with a clean finish.

name: Vietnam: Son La Select SHG, Son La
cupping_notes: Spicy, cocoa, and herbal notes with caramel and almond undertones; medium body, bright acidity; smooth finish with sweet aftertaste.
description:
Son La's mountainous terrain and ideal climate conditions create perfect growing conditions for the hardy Catimor varietal, which thrives in this region without requiring irrigation. The cooperative behind this coffee is committed to sustainable farming practices and social responsibility, led by manager Ngoc Anh Sprunker, who also serves as Vietnam chapter chairperson of the International Women's Coffee Association.


The washed processing method enhances the coffee's inherent brightness while maintaining its medium body and smooth character. This processing style allows the unique terroir of the Son La region to shine through, creating a cup that balances the spicy and herbal notes characteristic of Vietnamese highland coffees with the chocolatey richness that makes this coffee approachable for a wide range of palates.

Cupping Notes: Spicy, cocoa, and herbal notes with caramel and almond undertones; medium body, bright acidity; smooth finish with sweet aftertaste.

name: Bolivia: Apolo, La Paz Department
cupping_notes: Transparent citrus and tangerine acidity. With remarkable clarity, complexity, and creaminess.
description:
Apolo is an indigenous region of Northern Bolivia that had never commercially grown or exported coffee, until they began working with Wildlife Conservation Society and Cafe Kreyol in order to create a model of ecological preservation, and economic sustainable income. Cafe Kreyol works directly with 5 indigenous communities of Apolo, that speak a form of Quechua that predates the Incas.

The coffee from this region, with a very defined orange acidity (when roasted light), chocolatey flavor, and sweet aroma, is quickly taking it’s place as one of the top coffees of Bolivia. Cupping notes:Transparent citrus and tangerine acidity. With remarkable clarity, complexity, and creaminess.  Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Bolivia: Organic Caranavi FTO, La Paz Department
local_region:
Caranavi & Apolo Province
La Paz municipality
cupping_notes: Super sweet caramel, milk chocolate, rounded but heavy body with mild orange acidity in the undertones makes this an extremely balanced cup that is versatile and can be used for drip, cold brew, or a single origin espresso. Probably best roasted to a nice medium.
description: The Caranavi is a specialty Bolivian coffee grown by a small group of native coffee farmers known as colonials, on land that is at high elevations rising to 8,500 feet above sea level. These coffee beans are cultivated in the tropical growing regions of Bolivia below a magnificent forest, coming from 41 small farms averaging about three acres each. This cooperative is comprised of all indigenous people, that are native to the land on the edge of the Andes mountains and bordering the rain forest and jungle of the Amazon. The land next to their farms is government-protected land, and the indigenous groups have decided to treat their land as if it were protected as well. Most of which have already been certified Smithsonian Bird Friendly, and all of which have been certified Organic. Cupping Notes: Super sweet caramel, milk chocolate, rounded but heavy body with mild orange acidity in the undertones makes this an extremely balanced cup that is versatile and can be used for drip, cold brew, or a single origin espresso. Probably best roasted to a nice medium.

name: Brazil: Cerrado 17/18, Minas Gerais
variety: Mundo Novo, Yellow Catuai, Red Catuai, Acaia
cupping_notes: Walnut and dark chocolate notes that carry over into the cup with brown sugar; low acidity, medium body, a balanced cup.
description:
Cerrado is a region in the state of Minas Gerais, the largest coffee-producing state in Brazil. Coffee has been a major crop in this region since 1980's mainly because of the devastating black frost of 1975 that forced growers from the Parana region to relocate north to Cerrado and other areas of Minas Gerais.
Today, coffee is produced by over 4,500 growers on 175,00 hectares of farmland with yields of approximately 5.5 to 6.0 million bags per year. The coffee is grown in rich soil that the natives call "Terra Roxa" or "Red Earth" and other factors such as consistent rains, high daytime temperatures, and dry winters combine to make the Cerrado region ideal for producing coffee.

Cupping Notes: Walnut and dark chocolate notes that carry over into the cup with brown sugar; low acidity, medium body, a balanced cup.
Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Brazil: Decaf Mogiana, Sul de Minas
cupping_notes: Mild acidity and sweetness with an above-average body and notes of walnut and cocoa.
description:
COCAPEC, a cooperative known for its natural drying methods, delivers a decaffeinated coffee bean from the Mogiana region of Brazil––an area along the borders of Sao Paulo and Minas Gerais. The cooperative works in partnership with its members to guarantee consistent coffee quality. This coffee is dried in the sun and in a mechanical dryer to ensure the moisture evaporates.
 

Cupping Notes: Mild acidity and sweetness with an above-average body and notes of walnut and cocoa.
 
Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Brazil: Organic Fazenda Dutra 14/16, Matas de Minas
variety: Red and yellow Catuai, Mundo Novo, Bourbon, Catucai, yellow Pacamara, Icatu, Geisha, Catiguá MG3, Aramosa, Kent
cupping_notes: Sweet, medium brightness and body with notes of cherry, cocoa, and raisin.
description:
Brazil Organic Dutra Natural 14/16 comes from a family-owned coffee farm in Sao Joao do Manjuacu inland from the east coast of Brazil. This coffee has a delicate but citric acidity and a smooth body with notes of cherry, cocoa, and raisin
Cupping Notes: Sweet, medium brightness and body with notes of cherry, cocoa, and raisin.

name: Burundi: Twaranyuzwe Co-op, Kavumu
cupping_notes: Pineapple, jam, green apple, and cherry notes with tropical fruit brightness; very high acidity, average body; high sweetness with vibrant fruit-forward character and juicy complexity.
description:
This natural processed coffee comes from the Twaranyuzwe cooperative in Kavumu, Burundi, established in 2013 by smallholder coffee farmers. The cooperative's name translates to "we have understood," signifying the members' commitment to understanding and investing in the future of coffee. This lot represents the collective effort of 915 specialty coffee producers, 490 of whom are cooperative members.


The Bourbon varietal cherries are grown at elevations up to 1840 meters above sea level before being transported to the Kavumu Washing Station. Upon arrival, cherries are meticulously sorted to ensure only ripe red cherries proceed to processing. The natural processing method begins with pre-drying in the shade, where intensive sorting removes any remaining defects, followed by 10-15 days of sun drying on raised beds depending on weather conditions.


Harvested in April and exported in October, this coffee showcases the exceptional terroir of Burundi's highlands through its Bourbon genetics. The careful two-stage sorting process and controlled drying create a cup that bursts with tropical fruit character—pineapple and green apple brightness balanced by jammy sweetness and cherry depth—demonstrating why Burundi has emerged as one of Africa's most exciting specialty coffee origins.


Cupping Notes: 
Pineapple, jam, green apple, and cherry notes with tropical fruit brightness; very high acidity, average body; high sweetness with vibrant fruit-forward character and juicy complexity.


Try before you stock up with a half-pound sample!

name: CBC Blend: Ferdelance
cupping_notes: Roast it lighter and enjoy a creamy chocolate espresso drink with a dominant nutty taste, or take it to the limit and get the bittersweet end of the spectrum. Very versatile and quite complex.
description: Created by Russ as the perfect single shot, our own Ferdelance blend is one of our favorites for espresso––plenty of crema and lots of nutty richness. While its namesake isn’t precisely straight from Central America’s deadly viper, this mix is still high octane, and it packs a potent, complex bite! It's a potent blend (roasted dark), but also sweet when roasted lighter, and develops a thick crema. Deep and rich-flavored with heavy body and the balance you might expect from a blend so long in development. Roast it lighter and enjoy a creamy chocolate espresso drink with a dominant nutty taste, or take it to the limit and get the bittersweet end of the spectrum. Very versatile and quite complex.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: CBC Blend: Kope Kapuna
cupping_notes: Chocolaty and fruity, with a hint of spice and just enough heft and body to be truly satisfying.
description: Our Kope Kapuna combination was a 2007 Holiday hit. So rather than keeping the secret limited edition, we decided to make it permanent in our offerings. Russ concocted this bean combo with plenty of Hawaiian flair and nostalgia.  In keeping with the season's pleasures, the Kope Kapuna blend is chocolatey, fruity, and spicy. It’s perfect for a chilly holiday morning––and now, every morning! Year-round, this is our special offering to you. Add your roasting results and tips to the product reviews/comments for everyone to share.Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: CBC Blend: Red Sea
cupping_notes: Fruity characteristics of ripe red fruits with bright acidity and earthy undertones.
description: An exciting, complex blend of Ethiopian and Yemen dry-processed beans! These two counties rest on opposite sides of the Red Sea, hence the name of this delicious mix. For those who prefer lighter roasts, this coffee will show excellent bright, ripe red fruits and berries. Once pushed to second crack, the beans develop a unique roasted fruit profile due to their birthplace––the high mountains. Cupping Notes:Fruity characteristics of ripe red fruits with bright acidity and earthy undertones. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: CBC Blend: True Mocca Java
cupping_notes: Medium-bodied with dark chocolate and black pepper notes, cedar, blueberry, and herbal flavors.
description: After seeing dozens of fake “Mocha Java” on the market, John created the only blend that guarantees that iconic flavor––our True Mocha Java blend. As a mixture of naturally-processed beans and wet-hulled beans (a method exclusive to Indonesian coffees in which the mucilage and parchment stay on the bean to ferment only overnight ), this is a wonderfully-complex combo––truly the best of both worlds. Despite the hardship in both Yemen and Java, farmers still have a passion for keeping their spot as one of the best origins of coffee, and this Yemen Mocha bean comes from the Pearl of Tehma, owned by Fautoum Muslot. Indonesia boasts a strong government that dominates coffee production, ensuring every bean is up to par. Though far away globally, the farms that grow these beans are small but mighty. But how was the Mocha Java first created? In the 15th century, when Yemen coffee beans were shipped out across the Red Sea, the Indonesian sailors on board would meet their stop at the island of Java. So, in some instances, the beans would blend, and when people tasted the combination, they were immediately hooked––the first Mocha Java may have been an accident, but it was a happy one we enjoy to this day. Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!

name: Colombia: Decaf EA, Cauca
cupping_notes: Candy-like, with notes of tropical fruit and citrus; creamy body
description: Processed with ethyl acetate, (a sweet-smelling organic compound derived from sugar cane created during fermentation) this bean originates from the Andean region’s only decaffeination plant, Descafecol. The plant relies on pure water from the Navado el Ris (a snow-capped volcano resting on the border of Caldas and Tolima) and natural ethyl acetate from sugar cane plants in Palmira, Colombia. From the Trilladora Andes Mill, the coffee’s complete process involves machine-pulping, 6-8 hour fermentation, rinsing and patio-drying for 12 hours, and a final 6-hour drying. The Decaffeination process starts with steaming the green coffee beans at low pressure in order to remove their silver skins. Then, the beans are swelled and softened with hot water, which starts the caffeine's hydrolysis process––water causes the caffeine to break down and separate from the other compounds. In the next step, the ethyl acetate circulates through the beans until at least 97% of the caffeine is removed. Saturated steam is applied at low pressure to remove the ethyl acetate in the final processing stages. Coffee beans are then cooled to ambient temperature, surrounded by fans, and polished with carnauba wax, protecting the beans from humidity. The coffee beans are vacuum-dried in drums to ensure water removal and bring moisture levels to less than 12%. Cupping notes: Candy-like, with notes of tropical fruit and citrus; creamy body. Try before you stock up with a 1/2lb sample!!

name: Colombia: Organic Sierra Nevada FTO, Santa Marta
cupping_notes: Sweet brown sugar aroma, exotic well balanced flavor, clean sweet lingering rich aftertaste and rich smooth silky medium to full body with bright vibrant acidity, orange, raspberry, grapefruit, peach and chocolate notes.
description:
This Colombian bean is produced by the TAYRONACA Association, a non-profit organization with over 300 specialty cocoa and coffee producers, half of which are the producers that contribute to Colombia Sierra Nevada TAYRONACA Fairtrade Organic. The TAYRONACA Association has partnered with SENA (National Apprenticeship Service) to provide courses on agricultural practices, phytosanitary control and business management. They are also invested in creating sustainable economic development which maximizes resources, increases productivity and quality. The coffee is handpicked to ensure uniformity then the cherry is machine pulped and undergoes different drying methods: 50% is sundried, 20% on raised beds, and 30% is dried mechanically.

Cupping notes: Sweet brown sugar aroma, exotic well balanced flavor, clean sweet lingering rich aftertaste and rich smooth silky medium to full body with bright vibrant acidity, orange, raspberry, grapefruit, peach and chocolate notes. 

Try before you stock up with a 1/2 pound sample!!

name: Costa Rica: Finca El Rinconcito Black Honey, El Cedral
Costa Rica: La Minita Estate, Tarrazu
cupping_notes: Brilliant acidity, a medium body, and sweet flavors of orange citrus, caramelized sugar, and fine chocolate. It is impeccably balanced with a long, satisfying finish
description:
Each coffee parcel differentiates from its neighbors by having its own micro-climate, but the Costa Rica La Minta is not combined with any other farms in a cooperative scenario. Each coffee is milled by itself to maintain integrity, and workers only pick cherries when they have fully ripened. The farm uses acclaimed gardening techniques all by hand. 

The La Minita Tarrazu sports beautiful honeysuckle-like blossoms after the initial rain and its birthplace is considered one of the best coffee origins in the world. A Rainforest Certified Alliance coffee, these beans come with the guarantee of a better life for the people and land it comes from––better infrastructure, a more sustainable ecosystem, farming education, and gender equality. Estate owners watch over every process to ensure every bean you roast reaches perfection. 

Cupping Notes: Brilliant acidity, a medium body, and sweet flavors of orange citrus, caramelized sugar, and fine chocolate. It is impeccably balanced with a long, satisfying finish.

name: Costa Rica: Romelia SHB
